description: Founding discipline of The Kitchen; weekly performances + Wednesday open-house screenings of video work in the original Mercer kitchen.

From the founding period onward; key tenures include Rhys Chatham (1971-73, 1977-80), Arthur Russell (1974-75), Garrett List (1975-77), George E. Lewis (1980-82), Ann DeMarinis (1982-85), Arto Lindsay (1986-87). Foundational role in no wave era.
Launched by Mary MacArthur; producer Carlota Schoolman produced video works for television.
Founding dance program at the Wooster/Broome SoHo location.
Curated by Leonardo Katz; included Filmworks '82 (curator: Amy Taubin).
Year-round contemporary composition and experimental music programming.

program: New Music/New York → New Music America
description: Festival format launched as New Music/New York 1979; expanded to New Music America 1980 — became a model multi-city contemporary-music festival traveling to other host cities through the 1980s-90s.
Curated dance commissioning and process-driven dance presentation series.
Vegetarian buffet + evening of time-based art; conceived by ED Elise Bernhardt.
Music series during the Bernhardt / Singer transition era.
Interdisciplinary discussion / talks series founded under Tim Griffin.
Electronic music series founded under Tim Griffin.

program: Aluminum Music + Pioneers of the Downtown Sound
description: 40th Anniversary programs (Aluminum Music April 15-16, 2011; Pioneers of the Downtown Sound September 9-10, 2011).
Distributed presentation model during the W 19th Street renovation; programs at Westbeth Artists Housing (163B Bank Street) and partner venues citywide.

50th-anniversary capital campaign for the renovation of the W 19th Street building. Public goal: $40M (campaign site); ArchPaper reported $19M pledged at launch with a $28M-five-year framing at announcement.
